query: Labour leadership election 2026 nomination timeline candidates schedule results

{"query":"Labour leadership election 2026 nomination timeline candidates schedule results","model":"mako","response_time_ms":771,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Labour_Party_leadership_election","title":"2026 Labour Party leadership election - Wikipedia","content":"2026 Labour Party leadership election
At a final total of 94%, Burnham received the highest amount of MP nominations ever recorded in a modern Labour leadership contest,[citation needed] exceeding Gordon Brown who received 88% of nominations in 2007.
In February, Anas Sarwar, the Leader of the Scottish Labour Party, called for Starmer to resign as party leader amid the Peter Mandelson appointment scandal.
Starmer announced his intention to resign on 22 June 2026, four days after the by-election.
By 13 July he had received 349 nominations, and by 14 July he had received 369 nominations.
Archived from the original on 21 March 2026.
Reed was the only member of the cabinet not to nominate Burnham last week – Shabana Mahmood and Anna Turley, by convention, will not nominate anyone because of their positions as chairs of Labour's NEC and the party respectively."},{"url":"https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/7/9/uk-labour-leadership-nominations-begin-whos-running-and-how-it-works","title":"UK Labour leadership nominations begin: Who’s running and how it works | Elections News | Al Jazeera","content":"British Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s announcement last month that he plans to step down as prime minister and leader of the Labour Party has set in motion the process to select his successor.
Nominations for party leader officially opened on Thursday.
If Burnham takes over midterm as expected without a national election, that would make him the fifth prime minister in a decade to enter Downing Street without winning a general election as party leader.
According to the Lord Ashcroft survey, a majority of Britons now believe the next prime minister should call early elections within the next year or so with 40 percent preferring elections “as soon as practically possible”."},{"url":"https://www.pollcheck.co.uk/leadership-challenge","title":"Labour Leadership Election 2026: Andy Burnham Elected Leader | PollCheck","content":"Keir Starmer resigned and Andy Burnham was elected unopposed to replace him on 17 July 2026.
The result, the timeline, the candidates, and what it took to win.
To reach the ballot, a candidate must be nominated by 20% of Labour MPs - 81 of the 403-strong PLP - and then by 5% of constituency parties or three affiliates.
Burnham was the only candidate to be eligibly nominated; everyone else listed either endorsed him, ruled out standing or fell short of the 81-MP threshold.
Gordon Brown became leader this way in 2007, taking over as PM three days after being confirmed."},{"url":"https://labourlist.org/2026/06/timetable-for-leadership-contest-confirmed-by-nec/","title":"Timetable for leadership contest confirmed by NEC - LabourList","content":"The timetable for a contest to replace Keir Starmer as Labour leader has been decided by the party’s national executive committee.
After a meeting earlier today, the NEC agreed the timetable for the election following Starmer’s resignation on Monday.
Affiliated organisations, including trade unions, will have between 6pm on July 15 and 6pm on July 16 to make their nominations.
Should there be more than one candidate nominated, a ballot of members and affiliated supporters will take place between August 6 and August 27, with the result being announced on August 29."},{"url":"https://www.local.gov.uk/about/our-meetings-and-leadership/political-composition/local-government-elections","title":"Local government elections | Local Government Association","content":"This section includes details of when different types of authorities are due to hold local elections and guidance on the pre-election period.
The pre-election period for local authorities will start from the publication of a notice of election, this will be no later than Monday 30 March.
Scheduled elections are being held on Thursday 7 May 2026."}]}

Burnham was blocked from standing in the 2026 Gorton and Denton by-election (held on 26 February) by the party's National Executive Committee \"National Executive Committee of the Labour Party (UK)\") but won the 2026 Makerfield by-election on 18 June after the constituency's MP, Josh Simons, resigned to allow Burnham to stand. He is expected to become the Leader of the Labour Party \"Leader of the Labour Party (UK)\") on 17 July 2026, and is expected to become Prime Minister three days after, on 20 July 2026.","score":0.8029425,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://labourlist.org/2026/06/timetable-for-leadership-contest-confirmed-by-nec","title":"Timetable for leadership contest confirmed by NEC","content":"Jun 25, 2026 — The timetable for a contest to replace Keir Starmer as Labour leader has been decided by the party's NEC.","score":0.74961954,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://labour.org.uk/labour-leadership-2026","title":"Labour Leadership Election 2026 – The Labour Party","content":"> The National Executive Committee has agreed to the following for the forthcoming election for Leader of the Labour Party, following Prime Minister Keir Starmer announcing his intention to resign. Keir Starmer will remain Leader of the Labour Party and Prime Minister until the election has concluded. Any member of the Parliamentary Labour Party (PLP) may put themselves or one other Member of the PLP forward. ### PLP nominations. Candidates will require the nomination of 20% of the PLP members to stand for election. ### Affiliate nominations stage begins. Should a candidate not reach the threshold for affiliate nominations, CLP nominations will then open and candidates must receive the support of at least 5% of CLPs to be validly nominated. ## Stay up to date with the Leadership Election. Enter your details to hear the latest about the election and our wider campaigns. ### PLP nominations stage begins. Members of the PLP may nominate themselves or one other member of the PLP. ### Affiliate nominations stage ends.","score":0.72559077,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.pollcheck.co.uk/leadership-challenge","title":"Labour Leadership Election 2026: Live Tracker | PollCheck","content":"Tracking the contest to replace him: the timeline, the candidates, and what it takes to win. If only one candidate is validly nominated, a special conference on 17 July can confirm them unopposed; if the race is contested, members and affiliated supporters vote through August. To reach the ballot, a candidate must be nominated by 20% of Labour MPs - 81 of the 403-strong PLP - and then by 5% of constituency parties or three affiliates. A candidate must be nominated by at least **20% of the PLP - 81 MPs** - to be validly nominated. The threshold was raised from 10% to 20% at the 2021 party conference, so it is now the same for a vacancy as for challenging a sitting leader. If a single candidate is validly nominated, a **leadership special conference on 17 July** can confirm them as leader unopposed, with no members' ballot.","score":0.7077487,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/7/9/uk-labour-leadership-nominations-begin-whos-running-and-how-it-works","title":"UK Labour leadership nominations begin: Who’s running and how it works | Elections News | Al Jazeera","content":"*Former Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham is seen as the clear frontrunner. Labour MP and challenger for Leader of the Labour party, Andy Burnham, waves as he leaves after delivering a speech in Manchester, northern England, on June 29, 2026. British Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s announcement last month that he plans to step down as prime minister and leader of the Labour Party has set in motion the process to select his successor. The successful candidate will take charge of the governing party, which holds a strong majority in parliament, and will become the United Kingdom’s seventh prime minister in a decade. Here’s what you need to know about the Labour leadership selection process, including how it works and who is in the running. Starmer’s resignation means Labour must select a new leader, who will then be positioned to take over as prime minister.","score":0.65887916,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.73,"request_id":"b740b21f-f4fd-40fe-973b-e21d1651b467"}
